The people complained about their difficulty and danger in approaching God. God makes it clear here that the priests are to come near on their behalf. Aaron would understand not to be proud of his position when he considered the great responsibility placed upon him. Do not be arrogant, but have reverence. The greater the trust of work and power that is committed to us, the greater danger there is of betraying that trust. This is a good reason why we should neither envy others' honors, nor desire high positions.

All believers are spiritual priests, and God has promised to take care of them. Godliness has the promise of the life that now is. From this provision for the priests, the apostle shows that it is the duty of Christian churches to support their ministers. Improper support makes improper ministers. The priests were to be completely devoted to their ministry, not diverted from it or disturbed in it by worldly cares or business. Also, so that they might be examples of living by faith, not only in God’s providence but also in his ordinances. The best should be offered as the first portion to the Lord. Those who think to save themselves by offering God the refuse deceive themselves, for God is not mocked.

As Israel was a people not to be numbered among the nations, so Levi was a tribe to be distinguished from the rest. Those who have God as their Inheritance and Portion forever should look with holy contempt and indifference upon the possessions of this world. The Levites were to give God his dues out of their tithes, just as the Israelites did out of their increase. See in verse [number], the way to have comfort in all our worldly possessions so as to bear no sin because of them. 1. We must be sure that what we have is honestly obtained and in service to God. That food is best eaten which is first earned; but if anyone will not work, neither shall he eat. 2. We must be sure that God has his dues out of it. We have the comfort of our possessions when we honor the Lord with them. You will bear no sin because of them when you have given the best portion from them. We should give alms of such things as we have, so that all may be holy and comforting to us.
